destinations.evidence: The SPC signature crate delegates allowlist membership to the canonical precompile-address metadata.
Async direct verification uses the shared allowlist, while the optimized synchronous path rejects non-allowlisted requests instead of falling back.

equivalence_argument: Direct SPC verification is centralized on the shared allowlist; non-allowlisted async verification uses sidecar behavior and optimized sync verification rejects it.
notes: The shared predicate is imported through ritual_spc_signature, which itself delegates to ritual_precompile_addresses.
rationale: The SPC verifier removes local address constants and hand-written HTTP/LLM conditionals, routing async `verify_single` direct verification through the shared predicate and using the same predicate in the optimized sync verifier. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: SPC direct verification must use `is_spc_block_verified_precompile` to decide which precompile requests are verified with executor public keys; non-allowlisted requests use the sidecar path in async verification and are rejected by the optimized synchronous direct path.
suggested_falsification_tests: For every address in `ALL_SPC_BLOCK_VERIFIED_PRECOMPILE_ADDRESSES`, assert direct verification attempts registry/public-key signature verification; for an address outside the allowlist, assert `verify_single` uses sidecar behavior and `verify_parallel_optimized` returns an invalid-request error.
summary: Direct SPC verification uses the shared allowlist.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Assert every shared allowlist address takes direct verification, and an address outside the allowlist is rejected by verify_parallel_optimized.
title: Direct SPC verification uses the shared allowlist
